behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local scoring logic (token counting + threshold comparison) inside one analyzer. No required module handoff, real I/O, or model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the keyword-frequency analyzer directly with in-memory configs (positive/negative lists and thresholds) and representative reviews containing only listed words; assert exact sentiment outputs, including boundary counts at threshold edges.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generation quality from a context-interpreting language analyzer. Mocked outputs can trivially pass and hide real failures (generic explanations, missed grounding).
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer/model on fixed reviews and assert grounding signals in output (e.g., direct phrase overlap/quoting of salient spans from input) plus anti-boilerplate checks across repeated runs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Using a stubbed generator would not test grounding behavior, so Agentic is required.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic tie-break logic in the keyword analyzer using local configuration; real failures are algorithm/config-evaluation bugs within one component.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke analyzer with mixed-keyword inputs where positive and negative counts tie (and near-tie controls), varying tie-break configuration values; assert sentiment matches the configured rule exactly.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Sarcasm intent detection is model-understanding quality, not deterministic local logic. A mock would likely miss this real non-deterministic failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real explanation analyzer/model on a sarcasm set (including positive-lexicon/negative-intent examples) and assert outputs explicitly indicate negative intent with evidence from the text.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If this passed with mocks only, it would not prove sarcasm understanding.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is a module-orchestration check plus semantic consistency between deterministic score and generated explanation. Real mismatch risk comes from live explanation generation; mocks can hide direction drift.
behaviors.test_description: Run the end-to-end service path with real keyword analyzer + real explanation analyzer, assert response includes both fields in one payload, and verify explanation sentiment direction matches the computed score across diverse inputs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration alone validates payload wiring, but Agentic is needed to catch real explanation/score disagreement from model behavior.
